title: orchis sambucina subsp. laurentina
intro: Orchis sambucina subsp. laurentina, also known as the common spurge or lesser butterfly orchid, is a perennial orchid native to Europe and North Africa. It is believed to possess medicinal properties due to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is thought to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential applications in treating conditions like arthritis and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Orchis sambucina subsp. laurentina are typically harvested and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully confirm its effectiveness.
